name: Peru (Republic of Peru / República del Perú)
summary: Peru is an Andean South American civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code 1984 (Código Civil, Decreto Legislativo 295) Book III (Family Law), drawing on Spanish civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation. Parental authority (patria potestad) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 418-471, supplemented by the Children and Adolescents Code 2000 (Law 27337). The Supreme Court of Justice (Corte Suprema de Justicia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Family Courts (Juzgados de Familia). Psychology profession is regulated through the Colegio de Psicólogos del Perú (Law 23019/1980). Peru is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Children and Adolescents Code art. IX. Peru acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 August 2001.

statutory_framework.title: Civil Code Book III — Family Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code Book III on Family Law. Arts. 418-471 govern patria potestad and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Children and Adolescents Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children and Adolescents Code codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle aligned with UNCRC obligations.

statutory_framework.title: Colegio de Psicólogos Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute establishing Colegio de Psicólogos del Perú — among earliest Latin American statutory psychology regulation.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Justice (Corte Suprema de Justicia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Tribunal Constitucional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Colegio de Psicólogos del Perú
professional_regulators.role: Federal professional college for psychologists in Peru under Law 23019/1980 — among earliest Latin American statutory psychology regulation.

anonymisation_convention: Peruvian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Peru independence + San Martín + Bolívar framework
key_developments.description: Peru independence 28 July 1821 from Spain under José de San Martín. Subsequent Battle of Ayacucho 9 December 1824 substantively distinctive final-Spanish-American-Wars-of-Independence battle. Foundational pre-modern-codification framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Colegio de Psicólogos Law 23019 + Sendero-Luminoso conflict-start framework
key_developments.description: Federal statute Law 23019 of 1980 establishing Colegio de Psicólogos del Perú — among earliest Latin American statutory psychology regulation. Sendero Luminoso (Shining Path) Maoist insurgency began 1980 substantively distinctive Latin American 20-year-armed-conflict framework with estimated 70,000 killed affecting subsequent demographic and family-law-implementation framework.

key_developments.title: Civil Code 1984 (Decreto Legislativo 295)
key_developments.description: Federal Civil Code enacted Decreto Legislativo 295 of 1984 with substantial modernisation including Book III on Family Law. Arts. 418-471 govern patria potestad and child custody. Foundational substantive-family-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Peru ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + Fujimori-presidency framework
key_developments.description: Peru ratified the UNCRC on 4 September 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Alberto Fujimori (Cambio 90) elected President 28 July 1990 — substantively distinctive Latin American authoritarian-populist framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Autogolpe 1992 + Constitution 1993 + Fujishock framework
key_developments.description: Fujimori autogolpe 5 April 1992 dissolving Congress + Constitutional Court substantively distinctive Latin American self-coup framework. Constitution of Peru 1993 adopted 31 October 1993 by referendum. Substantive Fujishock economic-stabilisation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Children and Adolescents Code (Law 27337) + UNCRC-aligned framework + Fujimori-fall
key_developments.description: Federal Code enacted Law 27337 of 7 August 2000 codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle (art. IX) aligned with UNCRC obligations. Alberto Fujimori resigned 19 November 2000 — substantively distinctive Latin-American democratic-restoration framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession + Truth and Reconciliation Commission framework
key_developments.description: Peru acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 August 2001 — substantively significant Hague Latin American accession. Substantive Truth and Reconciliation Commission 2001-2003 framework substantially affecting demographic and constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Fujimori conviction + human-rights-jurisprudence framework
key_developments.description: Alberto Fujimori convicted 7 April 2009 for human-rights violations — substantively distinctive Latin-American leader-accountability framework. Substantive Inter-American Court of Human Rights jurisprudence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Code of Children and Adolescents amendment + Law 30466 best-interests-of-the-child framework
key_developments.description: Code of Children and Adolescents amendment via Law 30466 effective 18 June 2016 establishing parameters and guarantees for the best-interests-of-the-child framework — substantively distinctive Andean superior-interest-codification framework establishing 14 procedural and substantive parameters for interés superior del niño analysis + structurally distinctive Latin-American codified-superior-interest-of-the-child framework + foundational substantive Tribunal Constitucional and Corte Suprema jurisprudence on superior-interest-of-the-child framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory including 2024 Corte Suprema substantive register evolution and Hague Convention 1980 cross-border implementation framework.

key_developments.title: Peruvian political crisis 2017-2022 + recurring-presidential-impeachment framework
key_developments.description: Peruvian political crisis 2017-2022 substantively distinctive Latin-American recurring-presidential-impeachment framework — Kuczynski-resignation 2018, Vizcarra-removal 2020, Sagasti-interim, Castillo-self-coup-and-removal December 2022, Dina Boluarte continuing presidency. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory affecting subsequent family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Boluarte presidency consolidation + Defensoría del Pueblo institutional position
key_developments.description: Dina Boluarte presidency consolidation 2023 following December 2022 Castillo removal — substantive constitutional-democratic-context affecting institutional framework. Defensoría del Pueblo institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Children and Adolescents Code 2000 + Civil Code 1984 framework. Multiple Corte Suprema and Tribunal Constitucional decisions developing superior-interest-of-the-child interpretation. Substantively significant Andean institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Corte Suprema + Tribunal Constitucional — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Boluarte presidency
key_developments.description: Corte Suprema de Justicia and Tribunal Constitucional continue to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code 1984 arts. 418-471 + Children and Adolescents Code 2000 art. IX + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Dina Boluarte presidency since 7 December 2022. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Peru operates a Spanish-civil-law family-law framework — places Peru in the Andean South American civil-law cluster with Bolivia, Ecuador, Colombia.

Colegio de Psicólogos Law 1980 is among the earliest Latin American statutory psychology regulations within the corpus — places Peru in the early-Latin-American-psychology-regulation cluster alongside Costa Rica (1977).
Hague Convention 1980 accession 2001 places Peru in the Hague Latin American cluster.
editorial_notes: Peru jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full San-Martín-to-Boluarte trajectory: 1821-Peru-independence-+-San-Martín-+-Bolívar + 1980-Colegio-de-Psicólogos-Law-23019-+-Sendero-Luminoso-conflict-start + 1984-Civil-Code-Decreto-Legislativo-295 + 1990-UNCRC-+-Fujimori-presidency + 1992-Autogolpe-+-Constitution-1993-+-Fujishock + 2000-Children-and-Adolescents-Code-+-Fujimori-fall + 2001-Hague-Convention-1980-accession-+-Truth-and-Reconciliation-Commission + 2009-Fujimori-conviction + 2018-Peruvian-political-crisis-+-recurring-presidential-impeachment + 2024-Corte-Suprema-+-Tribunal-Constitucional-+-Boluarte-presidency.
Civil-law Andean South America (Civil Code 1984 Book III + Children and Adolescents Code 2000 + Colegio de Psicólogos Law 1980 — earliest Latin American + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2001).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code 1984 + Children and Adolescents Code 2000 art. IX + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Latin-American + civil-law + Andean-South-American (with Bolivia, Ecuador, Colombia) + early-Latin-American-psychology-regulation-Law-23019-1980 (with Costa Rica 1977) + Sendero-Luminoso-Maoist-insurgency-1980-2000-70000-killed-distinctive + Fujimori-autogolpe-1992-Latin-American-self-coup-distinctive + Fujishock-economic-stabilisation + Truth-and-Reconciliation-Commission-2001-2003 + Fujimori-conviction-2009-Latin-American-leader-accountability + Peruvian-political-crisis-2017-2022-recurring-presidential-impeachment-Kuczynski-Vizcarra-Sagasti-Castillo-Boluarte-distinctive + Hague-Convention-2001 clusters within the corpus.
